items.title: BofA lifts IBM target, sees strong cash flow despite near-term margin pressure
items.description: International Business Machines Corporation (NYSE:IBM) is one of the Trending AI Stocks on Wall Street. On January 13, BofA Securities analyst Wamsi Mohan raised the price target on the stock to $335.00 (from $315.00) while maintaining a Buy rating. Firm analysts see robust FCF outlook for IBM, along with higher-margin software mix; offsetting near-term headwinds. […]

items.title: The Biggest Risk to Your Stock Portfolio Is Not Buying AI -- It's Buying the Wrong Kind of AI
items.author: Robert Izquierdo
items.description: While the AI industry is projected to grow significantly from $255 billion in 2025 to $1.7 trillion by 2031, not all AI stocks will deliver exceptional returns. The article identifies tech infrastructure as a booming sector, while software companies must possess strong economic moats to succeed long-term. Key opportunities exist in AI-optimized data centers, semiconductor chips, and emerging quantum computing technology.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Strong revenue growth (62% YoY to $57B), dominant position in GPU market for AI systems, and diversified involvement across multiple AI frontiers including quantum computing bridge technology (NVQLink).

Demonstrates superior competitive advantage through proprietary ontology technology, achieving 52% YoY government sales growth, illustrating successful long-term AI business model with strong economic moat.
Q3 revenue declined 20% to $33.1 million, largely due to government spending cuts, indicating vulnerability and lack of competitive differentiation compared to peers like Palantir.
Q3 revenue declined 20% to $33.1 million, largely due to government spending cuts, indicating vulnerability and lack of competitive differentiation compared to peers like Palantir.
Leading quantum computing contender with 9% YoY sales growth, 30-year dividend increase streak, and credible roadmap to deliver fault-tolerant quantum computer by 2029.
Benefits from AI data center buildout as supplier of high-speed, reliable components essential for AI-optimized infrastructure expansion.
Positioned to benefit from rapid expansion of AI-optimized data centers requiring specialized high-speed, reliable components for massive computational infrastructure.
Provides diversified exposure to energy infrastructure supporting massive AI data centers, addressing the critical power supply needs of AI expansion.

items.title: Jim Cramer on International Business Machines: “It’s Inexpensive Relative to Its Growth Rate”
items.description: International Business Machines Corporation (NYSE:IBM) is one of the stocks Jim Cramer shared his thoughts on. Cramer praised the company’s CEO, as he said: “Let’s take them one by one, starting with IBM, which has become an incredibly strong performer with a stock that’s nearly tripled since late 2022 when it broke free from its… […]

items.title: Is quantum the next great tech revolution?
items.description: Investing.com -- Quantum computing could achieve "quantum advantage,” where quantum systems significantly outperform classical computers, around 2033, according to UBS Global Research in a recent note. However, the technology "has consistently been ’5 years away’ for the last 20 years," the report cautions.
